review:
The crypto market enters the week of June 21 in a confirmed bear phase with no actionable signal. The composite reads NO_ACTION with zero corroboration from secondary indicators, meaning the framework finds no dislocation worth exploiting - neither a sufficiently oversold setup to justify a long nor any short-side confirmation. Fear and Greed at 23 sits in extreme fear territory, yet funding rates running at a z-score of -0.7 suggest the crowd is already leaning short or neutral, which historically reduces the asymmetry available to a contrarian buyer. No change in regime occurred this week.

The one-month and one-quarter pictures are uniformly bearish and persistent. The phase distribution shows BEAR on every single observation day across both windows - 26 of 26 days over the month and 70 of 70 days over the quarter. BTC has shed 17 percent over the past month, with ETH and SOL slightly worse. Average composite scores in the low-to-mid 40s across both windows indicate a market that is weak but not yet in the deeply washed-out capitulation zone that would generate high-conviction BUY signals. The five BUY triggers observed over the month were minority events that did not persist, suggesting probing rather than a durable shift.

YTD context - bearing in mind the available data runs from late March only - shows the entire measured period has been a bear phase with no recovery to speak of. ETH underperforms BTC by roughly ten percentage points on a YTD basis, and the ETH/BTC z-score averaging -0.76 confirms a sustained rotation away from ETH. SOL/BTC at -1.47 on average is even more pronounced, suggesting risk appetite within crypto has been contracting toward the cycle's dominant-liquidity asset rather than expanding into higher-beta names. On a halving-cycle basis, the current timing would ordinarily sit in a pre-acceleration window, but the price action is not confirming that thesis.

Cross-asset context provides an interesting contrast. Equity markets are reading CALM - Business As Usual - with SPY up 1.5 percent over five days, VIX near its ten-year median, and credit spreads showing no stress. This means the crypto weakness is idiosyncratic rather than a symptom of broad risk-off. The Tepper dislocation lens would note that equity calm alongside crypto extreme fear creates a potential setup, but the absence of BTC score data and zero-corroboration signal argues for patience. A catalyst to watch would be a convergence of ETH/BTC z-score back toward zero and a funding rate normalization above -0.3, which together would suggest institutional rotation is resuming.
